What is Shiprocket Early COD(ECOD)? Should I opt for it?

* Online sellers in India largely depend on Cash-on-Delivery for purchases made from their website or through a marketplace. While everything about Cash-on-delivery orders is great, delayed payment makes business hard to grow. Shiprocket’s early COD helps to solve this problem by giving you control of your cash flow and receive payments with no hold.
* It's a great option if you want to recieve COD remittance .
* Get guaranteed remittance in just 2* days from the shipment delivered date.
* Grow your business by removing cash flow restrictions.
* Get full control over your remittance cycle and take better decisions for your business.
* Note: Once activated you cannot change or deactivate your plan for the next 30 days.

What are the plans or charges of Early COD (ECOD)?

* There are three different plans under Early COD:
   * Plan 1: Delivery + 2 Days
      * Opt for this plan if you wish to receive your COD payment within 2 days from the date of delivery. In this, shiprocket will charge 0.99% of your total COD amount. 
   * Plan 2: Delivery + 3 Days
      * Select this plan to receive your COD payment within 3 days from the date of delivery. With this plan, shiprocket will charge a transaction fee of 0.69% of your total COD amount.
   * Plan 3: Delivery + 4 Days
      * Get this plan to receive your COD payment within 4 days from the date of delivery. In this, shiprocket will charge a transaction fee of 0.49% of your total COD amount. 
* It's a great option if you want to take control of your money by receiving daily COD remittance.
* Get guaranteed remittance in just 2 or more days(based on your plan) from the shipment delivery date.
* Grow your business by removing cash flow restrictions.
* Get full control over your remittance cycle and make better decisions for your business.
* Note: Once activated you cannot change or deactivate your plan for the next 30 days.

How to activate Early COD(ECOD) for my Shiprocket account?

* Follow the below steps to activate the Early COD:

      * Go to ‘Settings’ >> ‘COD Payments’ >> ‘Early COD’.
      * You will navigate to the Early COD page where you can select your preferred plan.
      * To Activate any plan, accept the T&C and click on the ‘Activate’ button.
* Note: Once activated you cannot change or deactivate your plan for the next 30 days.

What is Shiprocket Postpaid? How do I activate it?

* The Postpaid plan is an effective way for the seller to get more transparent reconciliations, uninterrupted shipping, and benefit from a faster COD remittance process. It gives the seller more power over their money and enables them to utilize it for shipping while their COD remittance is processing.
* Benefits of Shiprocket Postpaid:
   * Dynamic Shipping Limit: With the Postpaid feature, a seller gets a daily dynamic shipping limit based on his/her COD delivered shipments. 
   * No worries about low balance or inability to recharge

* How to switch from Prepaid to Postpaid?
   * Go to ‘Settings’ from the left menu in your shiprocket account:
   * Select ‘Postpaid’ under the ‘COD Payments’ section.
   * Turn the toggle on to switch from Prepaid to Postpaid. 
   * Click on ‘Submit’ to proceed.
* You can also, connect with your Key Account Manager for switching from Prepaid to Postpaid.

What are the necessary steps to be fulfilled for hassle free COD remittance?

* To get a hassle-free COD remittance, ensure the following:
   * Bank details are updated in your Shiprocket panel in which you want the amount to get remitted.
   * Your Shiprocket wallet balance is always positive (only for prepaid sellers).
   * There is no freight invoice pending for more than 30 days.

How to check how much COD is pending to be remitted or how to check my cod remittance on shiprocket?
* In Shiprocket, you can easily view the COD amount that is pending to be remitted.
* Follow these steps:
   * Go to ‘[Finances]( from the left menu in your shiprocket account.
   * Click on ‘COD Remittance’ from the top section.
   * Here, you can see the sections related to COD Remittance, mentioned below:
      * COD To Be Remitted: Amount to be remitted in the next cycle.
      * Last COD Remitted: Amount remitted most recently.
      * Total COD Remitted: Amount that has been remitted in the last 30 days.
      * Total deduction from COD: It includes the Freight charges, Early COD, and RTO Reversal Amount.
      * Remittance initiated: We have initiated the COD amount. It will be remitted in the next COD cycle.
      * Pending RTO amount : RTO amount pending to be deducted from COD

When will my COD amount be remitted or transferred to my bank account?

* You can expect to receive your Cash on Delivery (COD) amount in your bank account within 8 to 9 days from the date of order delivery. 
* Alternatively, you can opt for our Early COD option, and receive COD payments in your bank account as soon as 2 days after delivery.

How do I recharge my Shiprocket wallet?

* Click on ‘Recharge Wallet’ from the top navigation bar on the seller panel.
* A pop-up will appear where you need to enter the amount you wish to recharge.
* You can enter your preferred amount for recharge, or you can make a quick selection from the options. Please keep in mind that the minimum recharge amount is Rs.200 and if you choose to enter the amount, it must be a multiple of 100, such as 500, 800, 10,000, and so on.
* Next, enter a coupon code if you have any or choose a coupon from the drop-down menu and click on Apply.
* Click on the ‘Continue to Payment’ button to proceed further.
* Next, select your preferred payment method (such as UPI, Netbanking, Card, or Wallet) and click on the Pay button. It will take you to your chosen payment gateway where you can complete your transaction. You can also recharge your wallet by making payment to a virtual account. You can find virtual account details on your invoices.

What happens if I don't pay my invoice?

* If a Freight invoice is pending:
   * Here, we hold the remittance(CRF's) amount that is either equal to or greater than the pending amount of invoice. We will transfer the pending remittance to your bank account as soon as you clear your invoice. Example: If an invoice of 10,000 is unpaid and the seller has 3 CRF's of 5,000 ,4,000 , 2,000 and 1,000. Then 3 CRF's 5,000 4,000 and 2,000 would be held and 1,000 would be remitted. We don't hold COD for pending VAS invoice
* If a SaaS invoice is pending:
   * If your invoice is still overdue after the last date, you get 10 extra days to clear your invoice. After this, your account will be downgraded You can then downgrade to our Lite plan or renew your existing plan as required.  
* If you have any further query, you can call our customer support team at +91-9711451060 or +91-9711541060

How to check the applied COD charges?

Follow the listed below steps to check applied COD charges:
* Navigate to the “Tools” from the side menu on seller panel and select Rate Calculator.
* Now, select your shipment type. Forward shipment is when an order is being delivered from you to the customer’s address. Whereas, Return shipment is when a customer buys a product but not happy with it and initiates a return and now, the shipment is returning back to the pickup address.  
*  Enter your Pickup area pincode which can be your office, home or may be warehouse address.
* Enter the delivery area pincode which will be your end customer’s address.  
* Enter the Actual Weight of your shipment. Actual weight is the final weight of your shipment including the product and packaging. It should always be in Kg. 
* We always recommend you add the dimensions of your shipment as volumetric weight defines the space a shipment occupies and therefore gives you a more accurate rate of your shipment. 
* For payment type, select COD. 
* Click on the calculate button and check the results. 
* Go to the Shipment Rate column and hover on the info icon to check the bifurcation of Freight and COD charges.
For COD shipments, a fixed COD charge or COD% of the order value whichever is higher will be taken while calculating the COD fee.

Where can I see the shipping rates or charges for all couriers or a single courier?

We have a rate card that provides the shipping rates of all couriers in one place. Follow these steps to check the shipping rates:
* Navigate to the “Tools” option from the left menu and select Rate Card.
* Here, you will find the rates charged by all partner couriers of shiprocket for forward, reverse, and document shipments in each delivery zone.
* You can apply filters for a specific courier, mode of shipment, and weight to get more concise information. 
* You can also select a specific plan to see the rates under that plan or compare rates of different plans.

How to calculate the shipping rate for sending a product from one pincode to another?

Follow these steps to easily calculate your shipping rates:
* Navigate to the “Tools” from the side menu on the seller panel and select Rate Calculator.
* Now, select your shipment type. Forward shipment is when an order is being delivered from you to the customer’s address. Whereas, Return shipment is when a customer buys a product but is not happy with it and initiates a return and now, the shipment is returning back to the pickup address.
* Enter your Pickup area pincode which can be your office, home or maybe warehouse address.
* Enter the delivery area pincode which will be your end customer’s address.  
* Enter the Actual Weight of your shipment. Actual weight is the final weight of your shipment including the product and packaging. It should always be in Kg. 
* We always recommend you add the dimensions of your shipment as volumetric weight defines the space a shipment occupies and therefore gives you a more accurate rate of your shipment. 
* Select your Shipment Payment Type. 
* Enter the Value of your shipment.
* Select yes or no accordingly to acknowledge dangerous goods. Dangerous Goods are items that may endanger the safety of an aircraft or persons on board it as there are potential adverse effects of temperature, pressure and humidity variations.
* Now, click the calculate button to view the shipping rates of your shipment.
On the left side of your courier rate result, check your order details including your pickup pincode, delivery area, shipment value, payment type, and applicable weight in Kg.
Check the serviceable list of couriers, their estimated delivery dates, rating, chargeable weight, shipment rate, and applied COD charges here if you have opted for cash on delivery payment type. 

You can also create a shipment with the information you entered. Click on the create shipment button and you will be directed to the quick shipment form page where you can fill in the remaining information of your shipment and can save yourself from a lot of hassles.

My Bank Verification Got Failed. What Now?

After submission of details, Shiprocket team checks your entries within 24 - 48 business hours & approves or rejects your application based on what they find.
Here’s why your bank verification may have failed:
* Mismatched Account Numbers: Your account number and the 're-enter account number' fields must match. Any disparity between the two will result in a verification failure.
* Incorrect Account Type: Ensure that you select the correct account type that aligns with your business. Selecting the wrong account type can lead to verification failure.
* Incorrect IFSC Code: Make sure you enter the correct IFSC (Indian Financial System Code) of your bank branch. If you're unsure about the IFSC code, you can use the IFSC lookup option available in the Shiprocket panel.
* Discrepancies in Account Information: The account number and IFSC you provide must exactly match the information printed on your cancelled cheque.
* Unclear Photograph of Cancelled Cheque: When uploading a photograph of your cancelled cheque, ensure it is clear and legible. Blurry or unclear images can result in verification failure.
* Photograph of Passbook: If you choose to upload a picture of your passbook instead of a cancelled cheque, it should clearly display your bank stamp and your photograph, ensuring the authenticity of the document.
If your bank verification has failed because of the above reasons, we request you to submit your documents again. Our team will cross-check your details again and get back to you within 48 business hours.

What are Paid and Unpaid Freight Invoices?

* Freight invoice: Invoice for the payment charged for shipping your orders through ShipRocket. To see your freight invoice:
* Go to Finances  from the left menu and select bills from the drop down menu
* Here you can see details like Invoice date, Due date, Total, Status and Action.
* There are three types of status: Paid, Unpaid and Cancelled
1. Paid: Invoices for which you have already paid either from your ShipRocket wallet or externally.
2. Unpaid: Invoices still due for payment.
3. Cancelled: Invoice against the cancelled orders.
* You can click on View Invoice option to view a particular invoice

How can I pay my shipping invoice?

To pay your shipping invoice, please follow the steps mentioned below:
* Click on the ‘Finances’ section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Go to the ‘Bills’ ssection and check the invoices of Unpaid and click on the 'view invoice' option.
* Click on the ‘Pay’ option against the invoice number to process your payment.

Why do I need to add my bank account details?

We need your bank account details to transfer your COD amount.

I'm not able to recharge my wallet. Please help!

We are very sorry for the inconvenience caused. Please try again after some time. 
If the problem persists, we request you to contact our customer support team at +91-9711451060 or +91-9711541060.

My transaction is successful, but I haven't received money in my Shiprocket wallet

Usually, the money gets credited in the account within 24-48 hours. If the issue persists, we recommend you call our support team at +91-9711451060 or +91-9711541060 or write to us at support@shiprocket.in.

How to print eway bill number on shipping label?

Eway Bill Number: Under GST Act, It is mandatory to enter Ewaybill number for shipping of goods from one place to another, either interstate or intrastate and of value more than INR 50,000. You can easily enter your Eway bill number while creating a manual order in Shiprocket. To generate eway bill, please visit https://ewaybillgst.gov.in/Others/EBPrintnew.aspx
Note: The e-way bill number will be printed on the label and will be shared with the courier partner in the pick up API.

Where can I check my wallet history or recharge history?

*To check your wallet history:
   * Go to ‘Finances’ from the left menu.
   * Click on the ‘Wallet Transactions’ 
   * Here, you can view all your transaction history for your wallet.
   * Alternatively, you can select Recharge History to view all your wallet recharges.

How to check the Passbook?

To check the passbook, follow these steps:
* Go to the ‘Finances’ section from the left menu.
* Then, click on the ‘Wallet Transactions’  and then click on the passbook tab
* Here, you will find the ‘Current Usable Balance’, ‘Balance On Hold’, and ‘Total Balance’.
* Also, you can see the category-wise detailed descriptions of the transactions.

Finances section in the Shiprocket account

You can access the Finances section from the left menu on the seller panel. It provides all transaction-related information across the following sub-menus:

* Freight & VAS Charges: Displays order details along with shipping and value-added service charges.
* COD Remittance: Shows details of all COD transactions, including Instant and Future COD.
* Wallet Transactions: Includes Passbook (order-level transaction details) and Recharge History (wallet recharge records).
* Bills: Contains Invoices, Credit Receipts (extra credits such as weight discrepancy or lost package adjustments), and Debit Notes (additional debits applied to the account).
* Current Plan Details: Shows details of your active pricing plan and available services.
* International Shipping Bill: Displays transaction details for international shipments.

How to check the shipping transaction details?

* Go to the Finances section from the left menu on the seller panel.

* Click on Freight & VAS Charges from the drop-down submenu.

Here, you can view detailed information such as:
* Total Freight Charges
* Uninvoiced freight charges
* Total On-Hold Amount
* VAS charges

You can also view details against a specific AWB number or Order ID.

**How to check invoices in your Shiprocket account?**

To view generated invoices, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **Bills**, then open the **Invoices** tab.
* Here, you will find sections for **Freight Invoices** and **All Other Invoices**.
* Under **Freight Invoices**, you can view details such as **Invoice ID, Invoice Date, Due Date, Total Amount, and Status.

Can I recharge my wallet using UPI?

Yes, you can recharge your shiprocket wallet using UPI.

Can I use UPI or other payment methods instead of using Shiprocket Wallet?

No, You can only make payments using Shiprocket Wallet.

What is COD Reconciliation?

COD reconciliation refers to the process of reconciling cash collected from Cash on Delivery (COD) orders with the corresponding sales invoices or orders. In eCommerce and retail businesses, COD is a popular payment method where customers pay for their orders in cash upon delivery.

How to check the COD Reconciliation?
**How to check COD reconciliation?**

To check COD reconciliation details, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **COD Remittance** from the submenu.
* Here, you can view the following COD summary details:

  * **COD To Be Remitted:** Amount that will be remitted in the next cycle.
  * **Last COD Remitted:** Amount remitted most recently.
  * **Total COD Remitted:** Total COD amount remitted in the last 30 days.
  * **Total Deduction from COD:** Includes freight charges, Early COD charges, and RTO reversal amount.
  * **Remittance Initiated:** COD amount has been initiated and will be remitted in the next cycle.
  

Under **COD Remitted**, you will find two sections:

* **COD Remitted**
* **Upcoming COD**

Under COD Remitted, you can view details such as:

* Date
* CRF ID
* UTR
* COD Available
* Freight Charges from COD
* Early COD Charges
* RTO Reversal Amount
* Adjusted Amount
* Remittance Amount
* Remittance Method
* Status.

**How to check COD deductions?**

To check COD deductions, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **COD Remittance** from the submenu.
* Under **COD Remittance**, open the **COD Remitted** section.

Here, you can view details such as:

* **Date**
* **CRF ID**
* **UTR**
* **COD Available**
* Deductions : Deductions column  contain all the deductions against a particular CRF(cod) they can be : 
  **Freight Charges from COD**
  **Early COD Charges**
  **RTO Reversal Amount**
   * Invoice Adjustment

**How to check invoices in the Shiprocket panel?**

To check invoices, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **Bills**, then open the **Invoices** tab.
* Here, you will find two sections: **Freight Invoices** and **All Other Invoices**.
* You can view the generated invoices with details such as:

  * **Invoice Number**
  * **Invoice Date**
  * **Due Date**
  * **Total Amount**
  * **Status**
* To view a specific invoice, click **View Invoice** under the **Action** column.

**Note:**

1. Most companies have **one or two billing cycles**. If there are two cycles, invoices are generated on the **15th and 30th** of the month. If there is one cycle, invoices are generated on the **30th** of the month.
2. A **correct GSTIN and legal name** are required for generating **IRN on B2B invoices**.

What type of invoices  shown under the Invoices section?

You can view the various types of invoices under the invoice section:
* Freight Invoices:
   * SRF —> Freight invoice
   * SRW —> Warehouse/fulfilment
   * SRV —> VAS
   * SRX —> International
   * SRE —> Engage
   * SRC —> Early COD
   * SRM —> Manual invoice for Duty charges and service charge
   * SRD —> Direct
* All Other Invoices:
   * SRS —> SAAS invoice

**How to check Credit Receipts?**

To check credit receipts, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **Bills**, then open the **Credit Receipts** tab.
* Here, you can view credit note details such as:

  * **Note ID**
  * **Note Number**
  * **Note Date**
  * **Total Amount**
* To view a specific credit receipt, click **View Receipt** under the **Action** column.

Some of the important terminologies under the COD Remiitance.

RTO Reversal Amount: RTO reversal amount is the amount deducted against seller COD incase shipment status changes from RTO delivered to Delivered.
Adjusted Amount: Adjusted amount is COD amount adjusted against seller’s invoice. it can be any invoice. I.e., We deduct Legal and penal(fraud AWB’s) invoice from wallet if the wallet doesn’t have money we deduct it from COD.
Remittance Amount: The remittance amount refers to the final sum of money that is paid or remitted to a seller or vendor after all deductions and adjustments have been made. It represents the net amount owed to the seller, taking into account various deductions

What is the difference between Passbook and Wallet History?

In the Passbook, you will find transaction details associated with specific Order IDs and AWB numbers. On the other hand, Wallet History provides a record of the total amount transacted.

What are all payment methods available to recharge shiprocket wallet?

To recharge your shiprocket wallet you can use below payment options:
1. UPI
2. Debit/Credit Card (Visa, Master Card, RuPay, etc..)
3. Net Banking 
4. Wallet (PhonePe, Amazon Pay, Mobikwik, Freecharge, Airtel Payments Bank, Ola Money, Jio Money)
5. EMI (ZestMoney)

Can I enable state wise billing?

Yes. You can enable this feature by navigating to Settings > Billing > GSTIN Invoicing and enable the feature "Enable State GST involving". -->Make sure the box 'Enable Pickup Based Billing' is unchecked
enter the respective GST of each state and click on save.. You will get One invoice belonging to one State independent of the pickup location within that same state.
Check the box to enable Pick up address based billings i.e invoices would be raised based on pickup location not on the basis of state , there can be multiple invoices for same state

What is Secure Postpaid?
      
Secure Postpaid provides sellers with a credit limit based on their daily Cash on Delivery (COD) remittance. This limit is adjusted on a daily basis, ensuring that the credit available reflects the most current COD remittance figures.

What is Unsecure Postpaid?	

In Unsecured Postpaid, a seller is provided with a credit limit up to which they can make transactions. Offering the seller a predefined amount of credit for their transactions.

Does wallet amount goes negative in case of postpaid?	

Yes, the wallet amount can go negative up to the assigned limit.

Are there any alternative payment methods other than shiprocket wallet to make payments on the shiprocket panel?	

No, there is no alternative of shiprocket wallet. To make transactions on shiprocket panel, you need to recharge your wallet first.

What is the daily recharge limit?

There is no daily recharge limit with Shiprocket. You are free to recharge your account with any amount as per your needs.

What is the Minimum Balance for the Wallet?

There is no minimum balance requirement for your wallet. However, to proceed with shipping, you need to have a minimum balance of Rs.100 in your wallet.

How to Set On-Hold Charges to Zero        

When the weight discrepancy is resolved, the on-hold charges will automatically be set to zero.

Why Did I Receive a Lesser COD Amount Than Expected?

There could be several reasons why you received a lesser amount than expected in your Cash On Delivery (COD) remittance. One possible reason is that there might be a weight discrepancy raised against a shipment, which would put your wallet amount on-hold. The on-hold amount is the additional charge that the courier has applied against the discrepancy. By reserving this amount, we make sure that no one will use this amount until the dispute is resolved. Another reason could be that there was an incorrect payment made. Check for deduction's (ECOD, RTO reversal, Invoice adjustments,Freight charges).

Is Shiprocket provides the Invoice discounting?

No

Can I withdraw my wallet amount?

Yes, you can withdraw your wallet amount by following these steps:
* Go to the ‘Help & Support’ section from the left menu.
* Create a ticket by clicking the create ticket button.
* Select the category as Finance and the subcategory as ‘Transfer Wallet Amount into My Bank Account’.
* Your bank details and KYC details will get get auto - verified by the system.
* Submit the ticket. Our support team will verify the details and transfer your complete wallet amount to your bank account.

Note: Make sure you don't have any open shipments or a negative wallet balance. Also, Refundable amount= Wallet amount - In process shipments- Cashbacks. Except the first cashback all other cashbacks are deducted from the wallet proportionately.

How to deactivate the postpaid payment mode?

Reach out to your KAM or our support team if you want to switch from postpaid to prepaid mode.

What is the difference between Passbook and Wallet History?

In the Passbook, you will find transaction details associated with specific Order IDs and AWB numbers. On the other hand, Wallet History provides a record of the total amount transacted.

How can I get cashback for my recharge coupon?	

Once you complete your recharge, the cashback will be automatically added to your account based on the coupon used. There’s no additional action required from your side.

I want to reset the invoice suffix.

You can reset the invoice suffix by changing it, but ensure that it is not decreased.

What type of invoices  shown under the Invoices section?

You can view the various types of invoices under the invoice section:
Freight Invoices:
  1. SRF —> Freight invoice
  2. SRW —> Warehouse/fulfilment
  3. SRV —> VAS
  4. SRX —> International
  5. SRE —> Engage
  6. SRC —> Early COD
  7. SRM —> Manual invoice for Duty charges and service charge
  8. SRD —> Direct
All Other Invoices:
  1. SRS —> SAAS invoice

**How to check Credit Receipts?**

To check credit receipts, follow these steps:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **Bills**, then open the **Credit Receipts** tab.
* Here, you can view credit note details such as:

  * **Note Number**
  * **Note Date**
  * **Total Amount**
* To view a specific credit receipt, click **View Receipt** under the **Action** column.

How much will WhatsApp Communication cost me?

The price of this feature is also very affordable to perfectly fit within all sizes of budgets. You will be charged a minimal Rs 0.69 per message or an average of Rs 4.83 per order.(Prices are exclusive of GST & Non refundable)

I have already activated Shiprocket Postpaid, but it's not showing active for my account.

* If you're experiencing this issue, don't worry! Here's what you can do:
   * Email Support: Reach out to our customer support team at support@shiprocket.in. Our dedicated team will assist you promptly and help resolve any issues related to your Shiprocket Postpaid account.
   * Create a Support Ticket: You can also create a support ticket directly from your Shiprocket panel. Simply navigate to the support section and submit your query. Our team will prioritize your request and work to resolve the issue as quickly as possible.

How many times do I get freight invoices in a month?

We bill our customers twice in a month, therefore you would receive freight invoices at the mid and end of every month.

What do we mean by shipment forward charges and RTO charges?

Forward shipment and RTO charges are incurred on shipments, shipped through different courier partners.
Shipment forward charges: When an order is being delivered from seller to the customer’s address it is called a forward delivery and shipping charges incurred for the same shipment are called forward charges.
RTO backward charges: When a courier partner is unable to deliver the shipment to the end customer, it is marked as Return To Origin (RTO) i.e return the shipment back to the pickup address. The charges incurred for shipping the parcel back to the seller’s pick up address are called RTO backward charges.
To know about the details of forward and RTO charges for your shipments, you can refer to the rate calculator in the panel. To access the rate calculator, go to Tools and click on Rate Calculator from the left menu.

What happens if I don't pay my invoice?

* If a Freight invoice is pending:
Here, we hold 1.5x of your COD remittance. We will transfer the pending remittance to your bank account as soon as you clear your invoice. 
* If a SaaS invoice is pending:
If your invoice is still overdue after the last date, you get 10 extra days to clear your invoice. After this, your account will be suspended. You can then downgrade to our Lite plan or renew your existing plan as required.  
If you have any further query, you can call our customer support team at +91-9711451060 or +91-9711541060.

Why did you apply additional charges for weight and RTO on my account?

Weight Charges: You will see this charge when there's a difference between the entered and charged weight shared by the courier. However, you can always raise a dispute against this discrepancy. 
RTO Charges: All courier companies take a standard amount for RTO (Return to Origin) shipments.

What are Shiprocket Passbook Charges?

Here’s everything you need to know about Shiprocket Passbook charges:
Freight Charge- The forward shipment charge gets debited from your Shiprocket wallet at the time of order processing. 
Freight Charge Reversed - When you cancel an order or shipment, the forward shipment charge gets credited to your Shiprocket wallet. The amount will be reversed in case of courier reassignment. 
Excess Weight Charge - The on-hold amount gets debited when the weight dispute case goes in favour of the courier. 
RTO Freight Charge - Freight charges applicable on RTO shipments depending on its weight. 
RTO Freight Reversed - We will reverse the RTO freight charges if the status is revoked due to any reason. 
SR Credit - When Shiprocket gives you credit for multiple reasons.
Cancelled - Amount credited to your wallet in case of an order or shipment cancellation.
COD Charge - Depending on the courier, COD charges are applicable for all COD orders at the time of processing. 
Cod Charge Reversed - We reverse the COD amount in case your shipment goes to the RTO. 
Lost Credit - If your shipment gets lost in transit, we credit the applicable amount (Freight charges + total order amount up to 5000) to your wallet.
RTO Excess Weight Charge - The on-hold amount for your RTO shipment gets debited when the weight dispute case goes in favour of the courier. 
Damaged Credit - If the contents of your parcel are damaged during transit, we credit the applicable amount to your wallet.
RTO Excess Freight Reversed - When we credit the excess RTO weight charge to your wallet due to any reason.

Does Shiprocket charge any commission during COD remittance?

No, Shiprocket does not charge any commission for the standard COD remittance cycle, where the payment is transferred to you within 8 days of order delivery.

However, if you opt for Early COD or Instant COD, certain charges apply:
**Early COD Plans**
You can choose from three Early COD plans, based on how soon you want the payment after delivery:
* Plan 1: Delivery + 2 Days
Receive payment 2 days after delivery. A fee of 0.99% of the total COD amount is charged.

* Plan 2: Delivery + 3 Days
Receive payment 3 days after delivery. A fee of 0.69% of the total COD amount is charged.

* Plan 3: Delivery + 4 Days
Receive payment 4 days after delivery. A fee of 0.49% of the total COD amount is charged.

**Instant COD**
If you prefer to receive your COD amount one day after shipment, you can opt for Instant COD. A standard fee of up to 5% of the total COD amount will apply.
Example:
If ₹3,500 is remitted under Instant COD, the fee could be up to ₹175 (i.e., 5% of ₹3,500).

What is Partial COD in Shiprocket?

Partial COD (Collectable Cash on Delivery) is a hybrid payment model that allows customers to pay a part of the total order value online and settle the remaining amount upon delivery. This provides flexibility to buyers, reduces the upfront payment burden, and helps sellers confirm serious orders and lower cancellation risks.

What are the benefits of using Partial COD?

Partial COD offers multiple advantages: it helps confirm genuine orders by securing a partial payment upfront, reduces fraud and fake order risks, increases buyer trust through payment flexibility, and is especially useful for high-value orders where full upfront payment may be a barrier.

How does Partial COD work?

Sellers can define a fixed amount or percentage of the total order value to be collected online, while the rest is marked as COD. For example, if the order value is ₹1000 and the seller chooses a ₹100 online payment, the remaining ₹900 will be collected on delivery. This breakdown is visible in both order details and shipping labels.

Who can use the Partial COD functionality?

Partial COD is not available to all sellers by default. Interested sellers need to contact their Key Account Manager (KAM), Sales Onboarding representative, or support team to request activation. Once approved, the functionality is enabled for their account.

How can sellers enable Partial COD?

To enable Partial COD, sellers should reach out to their KAM, Sales Onboarding representative, or the support team. After approval, the Partial COD functionality will be activated for their account. Sellers must also ensure the correct label type (2 to 7) is enabled to display the collectable amount accurately.

Can sellers customize the Partial COD amount?

Yes, sellers can customize the upfront payment by setting a fixed amount or a percentage of the order value. This allows flexibility based on the seller’s business model and can be configured via their sales channel or API integration.

Is the COD amount visible to the buyer?

Yes, the remaining COD amount is clearly shown in the order details and is also printed on the shipping label. This ensures that both the buyer and the delivery partner are aware of the amount to be collected at the time of delivery.

How does Partial COD help in reducing order fraud?

Partial COD requires buyers to pay a small portion of the order value online. This upfront commitment helps confirm buyer intent, reduces the likelihood of fake or non-serious orders, and helps minimize cancellations.

What type of orders is Partial COD ideal for?

Partial COD is ideal for high-value orders where customers may be hesitant to pay the full amount upfront. It also helps in confirming genuine buyer interest for orders that carry higher risk of cancellation or fraud.

What label types support Partial COD?

To ensure accurate display of the collectable COD amount on shipping labels, sellers must use Label Type 2 through 7. Label Type 3 is commonly used for this purpose during setup.

How to add money in wallet or recharge my wallet?

To add money to your wallet or recharge your wallet, follow these steps on Shiprocket panel:

* Click on the plus icon next to your wallet balance displayed on the top header.
* Enter the amount you want to add.
* Add coupon if you have any and click on Pay.
* You will be redirected to payment screen where you can pay using credit card, debit card, UPI, netbanking and e-wallets.
* Once payment is completed, money will reflect to your wallet.

How does RTO work? Do I need balance in my account?

RTO stands for Return to Origin, which indicates that a shipment is being returned to the sender due to multiple failures in delivery attempts. Here are some common reasons for RTO:

* Incorrect Address: The address provided is not valid or cannot be located.
* Recipient Not Available: The recipient is not present to receive the package during delivery attempts.
* Refused Delivery: The recipient refuses to accept the package.
* Package Damaged During Transit: The package is damaged and cannot be delivered.

How RTO Works

* The courier marks the order as RTO when delivery fails (customer unavailable, address issues, refusal, etc.).
* The RTO charges depend on:
** Shipment weight
** Dimensions
** Courier partner
** Delivery zone

* Charges are deducted automatically:
You do NOT need to take manual action—RTO charges are auto-adjusted by Shiprocket.

For COD Orders
* RTO charges are deducted from your COD remittance. You will receive the remaining amount after these deductions.

For Prepaid Orders
* Since there is no COD amount to adjust from, RTO charges are deducted from your Shiprocket Wallet balance.

Ideally, its better to maintain wallet balance to avoid any delays in RTO order.

**How to download the COD charge/remittance report?**

Follow these steps to download the report:

* Go to the **Finances** section from the left menu on the seller panel.
* Click on **COD Remittance** {{https://app.shiprocket.in/sellers/remitted/cod-remittance?}} from the submenu.
* Click on the **Download** button available in the **top-right corner** of the page to download the report.

how can I reimburse tds from shiprocket?

Opt the 'I want to deduct tds payement' in the [Billing, Invoice and GSTin](https://app.shiprocket.in/sellers/settings/company-setup/billing-invoice-gst) tab of the settings page and Enter your TAN number under GSTin Invoice then click on save.

I have done my wallet recharge but amount not received?

You can raise a ticket for this issue from Help & Support section. 
* Go to Help & Support from left menu and click on Create Ticket.
* Select category 'Finance' and subcategory 'Issue in Recharging Wallet'.
* Our support team will reach out to you to resolve your issue.

How much time it take to make invoice of unbilled freight charge after delivery?

For any shipment, billing is triggered 3 days after it reaches its terminal status. You will be billed either at month-end or mid-month, depending on your billing cycle.

Are shipping charges inclusive of GST or exclusive?

Yes, Freight charges for shipping are inclusive of GST

If the order is cancelled, will I get a refund to my wallet?

If the order is marked cancelled on Shiprocket, the freight charges will get refunded to your wallet.
